items.title: GLM-5.2 pushed open long-context coding
items.oneliner: GLM-5.2 shipped with 1M context and open weights. Frontier coding pressure is no longer a closed-model monopoly.

items.title: Kimi cut coding token costs
items.oneliner: Moonshot says Kimi K2.7-Code uses 30% fewer tokens. Efficiency is becoming a product feature, not a benchmark footnote.

items.title: MaxProof raised the bar for theorem models
items.oneliner: MaxProof pairs generation, verification, and repair. Math models are moving from demos to competitive proof systems.

items.title: The US suspended Anthropic model access
items.oneliner: The US forced Anthropic to cut Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access for foreign nationals. AI export controls now bite.

items.title: Anthropic reversed hidden research guardrails
items.oneliner: Anthropic walked back invisible Fable safeguards after researcher backlash. Silent model degradation is becoming a governance red line.

items.title: FFmpeg was hit with 21 zero-days
items.oneliner: Researchers found 21 FFmpeg zero-days across core codecs and formats. If you process media, patching just became urgent.

items.title: Arch AUR malware spread widely
items.oneliner: More than 1,500 AUR packages were tied to a malware wave. Open package ecosystems remain a supply-chain soft spot.

items.title: AMD updater exposed an RCE path
items.oneliner: AMD's AutoUpdate reportedly fetched executables over HTTP, enabling MITM RCE. Updaters are still one of security's dumbest attack surfaces.

items.title: WASI finally got native async
items.oneliner: WASI 0.3 bakes async into the component model. WebAssembly apps can stop reinventing event loops per runtime.

items.title: Apple moved a font engine to Swift
items.oneliner: Apple rewrote the TrueType hinting interpreter in Swift and got a 13% speedup. Memory safety is now a performance story.
